data.title: Alinco Cuts Medium-Term Profit Targets as Geopolitics and Costs Bite
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Alinco Incorporated ( (JP:5933) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Alinco has revised down the financial targets in its Medium-Term Business Plan 2027, cutting its fiscal 2027 consolidated net sales goal from ¥68.0 billion to ¥65.2 billion and ordinary profit from ¥5.0 billion to ¥3.2 billion, which lowers its projected ordinary profit margin to 4.9% and ROE to 6.4%. The decision reflects solid expansion in its core ALBATROSS scaffolding business but significant pressure on its home and electronic equipment segments from geopolitical risks, yen depreciation, and higher resource and energy costs, alongside supply-chain disruptions from Middle East tensions, although the firm is maintaining its commitment to a payout ratio of at least 40% with progressive dividends.

The company remains focused on evolving its core businesses, reshaping its portfolio, and managing with a clear awareness of capital costs and stock price, in line with Tokyo Stock Exchange guidance for improved capital efficiency. By aligning its revised numerical targets with its latest fiscal 2027 earnings forecast, Alinco signals a more cautious but transparent outlook, aiming to preserve investor trust while acknowledging a tougher operating environment that could temper growth expectations for stakeholders.

More about Alinco Incorporated

Alinco Incorporated is a Japanese manufacturer whose core businesses span sales and rental of construction-related equipment, including its proprietary ring lock ALBATROSS scaffolding system, as well as home equipment and electronic equipment operations. Listed on the TSE Prime Market, the company has been positioning itself for medium- to long-term growth while pursuing capital cost-conscious management and a shareholder-friendly dividend policy.

Average Trading Volume: 77,728

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen19.86B

See more data about 5933 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Expects Change in Major Shareholders After CCEC Share Sale
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Holdings ( (JP:2579) ) has provided an update.

Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Holdings has announced an expected change in its major shareholders, following a decision by The Coca-Cola Export Corporation, a wholly owned subsidiary of The Coca-Cola Company, to sell back 4,605,300 shares it holds in the Japanese bottler. The transaction is scheduled to be settled on May 11, 2026, and will reduce CCEC’s stake from 11.22 percent to 8.44 percent of voting rights, lowering its rank from the second- to the third-largest shareholder.

The share sale stems from a prior over-the-counter share purchase agreement between CCEC and a financial instruments business operator, under which CCEC is now confirming the agreed sell-back of shares that were not repurchased. While this will formally cause CCEC to cease being classified as a major shareholder under the company’s criteria, Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Holdings says it expects no impact on its management structure or business performance, suggesting limited operational or strategic disruption for investors and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:2579) stock is a Buy with a Yen3400.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Holdings stock, see the JP:2579 Stock Forecast page.

More about Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Holdings

Coca-Cola Bottlers Japan Holdings, Inc. is a major beverage company in Japan, operating as part of the Coca-Cola system and focusing on the production, distribution and sale of Coca-Cola-branded soft drinks and related beverages. The company serves the Japanese market through a broad bottling and logistics network, supporting both retail and vending channels for non-alcoholic refreshments.

Average Trading Volume: 844,490

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen569.4B

For a thorough assessment of 2579 stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Meiwa Corrects Full-Year Net Income Figures After Minor Disclosure Error
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Meiwa Corporation ( (JP:8103) ).

Meiwa Corporation has issued a partial correction to its recently released notice on differences between full-year non-consolidated results and the previous fiscal year’s results, clarifying that earlier figures for net income and related change metrics contained errors. The company stresses that its official earnings summary remains accurate, with the corrected data showing fiscal 2026 net income of ¥2,711 million, a slightly larger year-on-year decline than first stated, which modestly alters the perceived scale of profit contraction but does not change the overall downward earnings trend.

The revision adjusts the year-on-year drop in net income from ¥1,385 million to ¥1,409 million and the decline rate from 33.6% to 34.2%, leaving net sales and operating and ordinary income figures unchanged from the prior notice. While limited in scope, this correction underscores Meiwa’s effort to maintain precise disclosure standards for investors and highlights the sensitivity of market participants to even small changes in profit figures, particularly in a year of weaker sales and reduced profitability compared with fiscal 2025.

More about Meiwa Corporation

Meiwa Corporation, listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange under code 8103, operates in Japan as a trading and manufacturing company, though this disclosure centers on its financial reporting. Led by representative Takashi Yoshida, the company reports non-consolidated results and communicates regularly with investors on its full-year performance and earnings trends.

Average Trading Volume: 797,905

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen33.46B

Learn more about 8103 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The latest announcement is out from WAM Global Ltd. ( (AU:WGB) ).

WAM Global Ltd. has reported a minor change in the shareholdings of director Kate Thorley, reflecting her participation in the company’s dividend reinvestment plan. Thorley acquired 82 additional ordinary shares directly, increasing her direct holding to 2,852 shares while her indirect holding through superannuation and investment entities remains at 108,359 shares.

The transaction was executed via the dividend reinvestment plan at an issue price of about $2.18 per share, with a total cash value of $178.50 attributed to the new allocation. The company confirmed there were no related changes in director interests in contracts and that the trade did not occur during a closed period that would have required prior written clearance.

More about WAM Global Ltd.

WAM Global Ltd. is an investment company listed on the ASX, operating in the funds management sector. It provides investors with exposure to a portfolio of global equities, aiming to generate capital growth and income through active management of listed international securities.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Balfour Beatty ( (GB:BBY) ) is now available.

Balfour Beatty has reported that as of 30 April 2026 its issued share capital consists of 492,848,584 ordinary shares, of which 6,394,302 are held in treasury and do not carry voting or dividend rights. This leaves a total of 486,454,282 voting rights, a figure the company has highlighted as the key denominator for shareholders and other investors when determining and disclosing changes to their holdings under UK transparency rules.

The update is a routine disclosure under Financial Conduct Authority requirements but provides clarity for investors on the company’s current capital structure and voting base. By confirming the number of voting rights and treasury shares, Balfour Beatty helps ensure accurate regulatory notifications, aiding transparency in its shareholder register and supporting orderly trading in its shares.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:BBY) stock is a Buy with a £900.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Balfour Beatty stock, see the GB:BBY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BBY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BBY is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial cash generation and clear positive technical momentum. It is tempered by thin/pressured operating margins and higher leverage, while valuation appears reasonable but not notably cheap and the dividend is modest.

To see Spark’s full report on BBY stock, click here.

More about Balfour Beatty

Balfour Beatty is a leading international infrastructure group with around 27,000 employees, focused on financing, developing, building, maintaining and operating complex and critical infrastructure that underpins national economies and local communities. With a history of more than a century, it is involved in landmark projects worldwide, including the Hinkley Point C nuclear power station in the UK, the Lyric Theatre in Hong Kong and the Automated People Mover at Los Angeles International Airport.
